timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Minoan civilization emerges on Crete
description: Complex palace-centered society develops on Crete, marking one of the earliest advanced civilizations in the Aegean world.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Mycenaean civilization rises
description: Mycenaean Greek kingdoms become the dominant power on mainland Greece and shape the later heroic traditions of Greek culture.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Mycenaeans take control of Crete
description: Mycenaean influence spreads into Crete after the decline of Minoan palace power.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Mycenaean palace systems collapse
description: The breakdown of palace society ushers in a period of disruption often called the Greek Dark Age.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Greek city-states consolidate
description: Independent poleis such as Athens, Sparta, and Corinth emerge as the central political form of Greek life.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Traditional date of the first Olympic Games
description: Greek tradition places the first Olympic festival at Olympia in 776 BCE, providing a famous chronological marker for Greek history.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Greek colonization expands across the Mediterranean
description: Greek settlers establish colonies from the Black Sea to southern Italy, extending trade and culture far beyond the Aegean.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Hoplite warfare becomes widespread
description: Citizen infantry organized in phalanx formations reshapes military and political life in many city-states.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Solon introduces reforms at Athens
description: Solon's legislation restructures debt, citizenship, and political participation, laying foundations for later Athenian democracy.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Persia conquers Lydia and the Greek cities of Ionia
description: Achaemenid control over the Ionian Greeks brings the eastern Greek world into the Persian Empire.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Cleisthenes reforms the Athenian constitution
description: Cleisthenes reorganizes the citizen body and institutions of Athens in a decisive step toward democracy.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Ionian Revolt begins
description: Greek cities in Asia Minor rebel against Persian rule, helping trigger the larger Greco-Persian conflicts.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Athenians defeat Persia at Marathon
description: The Athenian victory at Marathon becomes one of the defining moments of the Persian Wars.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Persian invasion reaches its height
description: The battles of Thermopylae, Salamis, and related campaigns mark the climactic stage of Xerxes I's invasion of Greece.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Greek victory at Plataea ends the Persian threat
description: The defeat of Persian forces at Plataea confirms the failure of Persia's campaign in mainland Greece.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Delian League is founded
description: Athens leads an alliance of Greek states that gradually develops into an Athenian maritime empire.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Peloponnesian War begins
description: War breaks out between Athens and Sparta, drawing much of the Greek world into a prolonged struggle.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Athens surrenders to Sparta
description: The Peloponnesian War ends with Spartan victory and the collapse of the Athenian empire.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Philip II wins the Battle of Chaeronea
description: Macedon defeats the combined forces of Athens and Thebes, ending the political independence of the major Greek city-states.

timeline_description: Major political, cultural, and military milestones in the history of ancient Greek civilization from the Bronze Age to the rise of Macedon.
title: Death of Alexander the Great
description: Alexander's death marks the conventional end of the Classical Greek age and the beginning of the Hellenistic era.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Birth of Jesus of Nazareth
description: Jesus of Nazareth is born in Roman Judea, beginning the life at the center of Christianity.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Crucifixion of Jesus
description: Jesus is executed by crucifixion under Roman authority, a foundational event in Christian belief.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Earliest Christian community forms in Jerusalem
description: Jesus's followers organize in Jerusalem and begin proclaiming his resurrection.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Conversion of Paul
description: Paul's conversion leads to missionary activity that helps spread Christianity across the Roman world.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Council of Jerusalem addresses Gentile converts
description: Early Christian leaders decide that non-Jewish converts need not fully adopt Jewish law, aiding expansion beyond Judaism.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Neronian persecution follows the Great Fire of Rome
description: Christians in Rome are persecuted under Nero after the city's devastating fire.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Destruction of the Second Temple
description: The Roman destruction of Jerusalem's Temple accelerates the separation of Christianity from Judaism.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Edict of Milan legalizes Christianity
description: Constantine and Licinius proclaim religious toleration, ending major imperial persecution of Christians.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: First Council of Nicaea meets
description: Bishops gather at Nicaea and issue a creed that becomes central to orthodox Christian doctrine.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Christianity becomes the Roman Empire's official religion
description: The Edict of Thessalonica establishes Nicene Christianity as the imperial state religion.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Augustine begins writing The City of God
description: Augustine's major theological work helps shape Western Christian thought in late antiquity and the Middle Ages.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Council of Chalcedon defines Christological doctrine
description: The council issues a major doctrinal statement on the nature of Christ, influencing later divisions within Christianity.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Gregory I becomes pope
description: Gregory the Great strengthens papal authority and shapes the medieval Latin Church.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Charlemagne is crowned emperor
description: The coronation of Charlemagne in Rome reinforces ties between the papacy and western Christian monarchy.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Great Schism divides eastern and western churches
description: The long-developing break between the Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches becomes formalized.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: First Crusade is proclaimed
description: Pope Urban II calls for a military expedition to the Holy Land, linking Latin Christianity to crusading warfare.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Martin Luther launches the Reformation
description: Luther's challenge to church practice sparks the Protestant Reformation and enduring divisions within Christianity.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Council of Trent begins
description: The Catholic Church opens the Council of Trent, defining doctrine and reforming practice during the Counter-Reformation.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: John Wesley's Aldersgate experience energizes Methodism
description: Wesley's conversion experience becomes a key moment in the evangelical revival associated with Methodism.

timeline_description: Major events in the development of Christianity from its origins in Roman Judea to the modern global church.
title: Second Vatican Council opens
description: Vatican II begins a major period of renewal in the Roman Catholic Church with worldwide consequences.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Napoleon begins the Italian campaign
description: Napoleon Bonaparte takes command in Italy and wins a series of victories that make him France's leading general.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Treaty of Campo Formio is signed
description: Austria makes peace with France after defeats in Italy, confirming French gains and Napoleon's prestige.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: French invade Egypt
description: Napoleon launches the Egyptian expedition in an effort to weaken British influence and expand French power.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Battle of the Nile cripples the French fleet
description: Horatio Nelson destroys much of the French fleet at Aboukir Bay, isolating Napoleon's army in Egypt.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Coup of 18 Brumaire brings Napoleon to power
description: Napoleon overthrows the Directory and becomes First Consul, linking French government directly to his military authority.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Napoleon wins the Battle of Marengo
description: The French victory over Austria restores French dominance in Italy and strengthens Napoleon's regime.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Treaty of Lunéville confirms French gains
description: Austria again makes peace with France, extending the settlement that followed Marengo.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Peace of Amiens briefly pauses the war
description: Britain and France agree to a temporary peace, ending the War of the Second Coalition.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: War resumes between Britain and France
description: The collapse of the Peace of Amiens restarts hostilities and opens the main phase of the Napoleonic Wars.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Napoleon crowns himself emperor
description: Napoleon becomes emperor of the French, consolidating personal rule while the European conflict deepens.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Nelson wins the Battle of Trafalgar
description: British naval victory at Trafalgar secures command of the seas and ends the threat of a French invasion of Britain.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Napoleon wins the Battle of Austerlitz
description: The defeat of Austria and Russia at Austerlitz becomes one of Napoleon's greatest victories.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Prussia is defeated at Jena and Auerstedt
description: Napoleon's army shatters Prussian resistance and expands French control in central Europe.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Treaties of Tilsit reshape Europe
description: Napoleon and Tsar Alexander I conclude agreements that leave France dominant on the continent.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Peninsular War begins
description: Resistance to French occupation in Spain and Portugal opens a long and costly theater of war.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Battle of Wagram restores French supremacy
description: Napoleon defeats Austria again at Wagram after renewed war in central Europe.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: French invasion of Russia begins
description: Napoleon leads the Grande Armée into Russia in a campaign that becomes a turning point of the wars.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Napoleon is defeated at Leipzig
description: The Battle of Leipzig, or Battle of Nations, decisively weakens French power in Germany.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Napoleon abdicates and goes to Elba
description: Allied armies enter Paris and force Napoleon to abdicate, ending his first reign.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, battles, and political turning points in the Napoleonic Wars from Napoleon's rise to Waterloo.
title: Battle of Waterloo ends Napoleon's rule
description: Napoleon's final defeat at Waterloo ends the Hundred Days and closes the Napoleonic Wars.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: February Revolution topples the tsar
description: Mass unrest and military mutiny force Nicholas II to abdicate, ending the Romanov monarchy.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Bolsheviks seize power in the October Revolution
description: Lenin's Bolsheviks overthrow the Provisional Government and establish a revolutionary regime.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Treaty of Brest-Litovsk is signed
description: Soviet Russia exits World War I at heavy territorial cost to secure the revolution at home.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Romanov family is executed
description: Nicholas II and his family are killed during the civil war, closing the dynastic chapter of imperial Russia.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: New Economic Policy is introduced
description: Lenin retreats from war communism and allows limited market activity to stabilize the economy.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Union of Soviet Socialist Republics is formed
description: Russia, Ukraine, Belarus, and the Transcaucasian federation create the USSR as a federal socialist state.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Lenin dies
description: Lenin's death opens the struggle for leadership that eventually brings Stalin to dominance.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: First Five-Year Plan begins
description: Stalin launches rapid industrialization and centralized economic planning on a massive scale.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Forced collectivization accelerates
description: The Soviet government compels peasants into collective farms, transforming agriculture through coercion.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Great Purge begins
description: Stalin's regime intensifies arrests, show trials, and executions across the party, military, and society.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Nazi-Soviet Pact is signed
description: Germany and the Soviet Union agree to a nonaggression pact with secret protocols dividing eastern Europe.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Germany invades the Soviet Union
description: Operation Barbarossa opens the main eastern front of World War II and threatens Soviet survival.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Battle of Stalingrad begins
description: The prolonged struggle for Stalingrad becomes a decisive turning point on the Eastern Front.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Soviet victory in Europe helps end World War II
description: The Red Army captures Berlin as Nazi Germany collapses in Europe.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Soviet Union tests its first atomic bomb
description: The successful Soviet atomic test ends the United States nuclear monopoly.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Khrushchev denounces Stalin
description: Khrushchev's secret speech launches de-Stalinization and shakes communist politics across the bloc.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Sputnik 1 is launched
description: The Soviet Union places the first artificial satellite in orbit, opening the space age.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human in space
description: Gagarin's orbital flight delivers a major symbolic triumph for the Soviet Union.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Mikhail Gorbachev comes to power
description: Gorbachev begins reforms associated with perestroika and glasnost in an effort to revive the Soviet system.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and social developments in the history of the Soviet Union from revolution to dissolution.
title: Soviet Union dissolves
description: The USSR formally ceases to exist after the failure of central authority and the declaration of independent republics.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Avicenna completes The Canon of Medicine
description: Avicenna's synthesis becomes one of the most influential medical texts of the medieval and early modern worlds.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Black Death transforms medical practice
description: The pandemic exposes the limits of medieval medicine and stimulates new public-health responses, including quarantine.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Vesalius publishes De humani corporis fabrica
description: Vesalius's anatomical work challenges inherited authority through direct observation and dissection.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Harvey describes the circulation of blood
description: William Harvey's work establishes circulation as a central principle of human physiology.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Jenner introduces smallpox vaccination
description: Edward Jenner demonstrates a method of protection against smallpox that becomes a landmark in preventive medicine.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Ether anesthesia is publicly demonstrated
description: Successful public demonstration of ether anesthesia transforms the possibilities of surgery.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Semmelweis advocates handwashing in obstetrics
description: Ignaz Semmelweis links hand hygiene to reduced maternal deaths, anticipating modern infection control.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: John Snow investigates the Broad Street cholera outbreak
description: Snow's work in London becomes a foundational episode in epidemiology and modern public health.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Lister promotes antiseptic surgery
description: Joseph Lister applies antiseptic methods to surgery, greatly reducing postoperative infection.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Ether anesthesia is publicly demonstrated
description: Successful public demonstration of ether anesthesia transforms the possibilities of surgery.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Semmelweis advocates handwashing in obstetrics
description: Ignaz Semmelweis links hand hygiene to reduced maternal deaths, anticipating modern infection control.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: John Snow investigates the Broad Street cholera outbreak
description: Snow's work in London becomes a foundational episode in epidemiology and modern public health.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Lister promotes antiseptic surgery
description: Joseph Lister applies antiseptic methods to surgery, greatly reducing postoperative infection.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Koch identifies the tuberculosis bacillus
description: Robert Koch isolates the bacterium causing tuberculosis, strengthening germ theory and bacteriology.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Röntgen discovers X-rays
description: The discovery of X-rays opens a new era in diagnostic imaging.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Fleming discovers penicillin
description: Alexander Fleming observes the antibacterial effect of penicillin, paving the way for antibiotic therapy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: Watson and Crick publish the structure of DNA
description: The double-helix model helps transform medicine by linking heredity, disease, and molecular biology.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: First successful human heart transplant is performed
description: Christiaan Barnard carries out the first successful human heart transplant, expanding the possibilities of organ transplantation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: First IVF birth occurs
description: The birth of Louise Brown marks a major advance in reproductive medicine.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Medicine
timeline_description: Major milestones in the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of disease from antiquity to the modern era.
title: COVID-19 pandemic accelerates global vaccine medicine
description: The COVID-19 pandemic drives rapid worldwide deployment of new vaccine technologies and reshapes public-health systems.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Faraday observes semiconductor behavior
description: Michael Faraday reports unusual temperature-dependent conductivity in silver sulfide, an early observation of semiconductor properties.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Braun discovers rectification in crystals
description: Karl Ferdinand Braun identifies one-way conduction in certain crystals, a key semiconductor effect.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Pickard patents the crystal detector
description: Greenleaf Whittier Pickard patents a practical silicon crystal detector for radio reception.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Russell Ohl identifies the p-n junction in silicon
description: Research at Bell Labs clarifies the electrical behavior of silicon junctions that become central to later devices.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Transistor effect is demonstrated at Bell Labs
description: John Bardeen, Walter Brattain, and William Shockley help create the transistor, transforming modern electronics.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Bell Labs announces the transistor
description: Public announcement of the transistor signals a new direction for electronics beyond vacuum tubes.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Commercial silicon transistors enter production
description: Silicon transistors begin to displace germanium devices because of better performance at higher temperatures.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Nobel Prize honors transistor research
description: The Nobel Prize in Physics recognizes the inventors of the transistor effect and the importance of semiconductor research.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Jack Kilby demonstrates the integrated circuit
description: Kilby's work shows that multiple electronic components can be built on a single piece of semiconductor material.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Robert Noyce advances the monolithic integrated circuit
description: Noyce's planar integrated circuit concept helps make mass-produced semiconductor chips practical.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: MOSFET technology is demonstrated
description: The metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistor becomes the device architecture that later dominates integrated electronics.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: CMOS logic is proposed
description: Complementary MOS logic offers a path to lower-power semiconductor design with major long-term impact.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Moore's Law is articulated
description: Gordon Moore observes the rapid scaling of integrated circuits, framing expectations for the semiconductor industry.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Fairchild alumni found Intel
description: The founding of Intel creates one of the central firms of the semiconductor age.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Intel 4004 microprocessor debuts
description: Intel releases the 4004, a landmark single-chip programmable processor built with semiconductor integration.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Second-generation microprocessors accelerate adoption
description: More capable microprocessors help move semiconductor logic into a widening range of commercial devices.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: CMOS becomes central to chip design
description: CMOS technology gains broad importance because it enables lower power consumption and dense integration.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Foundry manufacturing model expands with TSMC
description: The rise of dedicated semiconductor foundries reshapes global chip production and fabless design.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: System-on-chip design becomes mainstream
description: Increasingly complex semiconductor integration places processors, memory, and functions on single chips for consumer electronics.

timeline_description: Major scientific and industrial milestones in the rise of semiconductors from early discoveries to the modern chip era.
title: Smartphone era drives advanced chip demand
description: Modern smartphones accelerate demand for highly integrated, low-power semiconductor devices and manufacturing scale.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Olmec civilization emerges
description: The Olmec become one of the earliest major civilizations of Mesoamerica and influence later cultures in Mexico.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Teotihuacan begins to develop
description: Urban growth in the Valley of Mexico leads to the rise of Teotihuacan, one of the great cities of ancient America.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Classic Maya civilization flourishes in the south
description: Maya city-states in present-day southern Mexico and Central America reach a peak of writing, astronomy, and architecture.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Tenochtitlan is founded
description: The Mexica establish Tenochtitlan on Lake Texcoco, creating the city that becomes the core of the Aztec Empire.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Aztec Triple Alliance is formed
description: Tenochtitlan, Texcoco, and Tlacopan create the alliance that underpins Aztec imperial expansion.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Cortés lands in Mexico
description: Hernán Cortés begins the expedition that leads to the Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Tenochtitlan falls to the Spanish
description: The capture of Tenochtitlan ends the Aztec Empire and opens the colonial era of New Spain.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Viceroyalty of New Spain is established
description: Spain organizes its North American possessions under the viceroyalty based in Mexico City.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Hidalgo issues the Grito de Dolores
description: Miguel Hidalgo's call to rebellion launches the Mexican struggle for independence from Spain.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Mexico achieves independence
description: The Army of the Three Guarantees enters Mexico City and Spanish rule effectively ends.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Federal republic is established
description: Mexico adopts a federal constitution, shaping the framework of the new state.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Texas revolt succeeds against Mexico
description: The loss of Texas deepens political instability and foreshadows further conflict with the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Mexican-American War begins
description: War with the United States erupts over Texas and the borderlands.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo ends the war
description: Mexico cedes a vast northern territory to the United States after military defeat.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Liberal Constitution is promulgated
description: The Constitution of 1857 intensifies conflict between liberals and conservatives in Mexico.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Maximilian becomes emperor of Mexico
description: French intervention places Archduke Maximilian on the Mexican throne during a short-lived empire.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Republic is restored under Juárez
description: The defeat of the French-backed empire restores republican government.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Mexican Revolution begins
description: Opposition to Porfirio Díaz triggers a revolution that transforms Mexican politics and society.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: Constitution of 1917 is adopted
description: A new constitution establishes important social, labor, and land reform principles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Mexico
timeline_description: Major political and cultural milestones in Mexican history from early Mesoamerican civilizations to the modern republic.
title: PRI loses the presidency after seven decades
description: Vicente Fox's election ends the PRI's long hold on the presidency and marks a major democratic transition.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Hitler becomes chancellor of Germany
description: Adolf Hitler's appointment begins the Nazi seizure of power that makes state persecution possible.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: First concentration camps are established
description: The Nazi regime opens early camps such as Dachau to imprison political opponents and other targeted groups.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Nuremberg Laws strip Jews of citizenship
description: The regime codifies racial antisemitism through laws that exclude Jews from full civic life in Germany.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Anschluss brings Austrian Jews under Nazi rule
description: Germany annexes Austria, extending anti-Jewish persecution to Austrian territory.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Kristallnacht attacks devastate Jewish life
description: Coordinated violence against Jews, synagogues, and businesses marks a major escalation of Nazi persecution.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Germany invades Poland
description: The conquest of Poland places millions of Jews under Nazi control and opens the European war.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Nazi euthanasia program begins
description: The regime launches programs to murder people with disabilities, applying methods later used in extermination centers.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Auschwitz camp is established
description: The SS establishes Auschwitz, which later becomes the largest complex of concentration and killing camps.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Warsaw Ghetto is sealed
description: German authorities confine hundreds of thousands of Jews in the Warsaw Ghetto under lethal conditions.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Invasion of the Soviet Union unleashes mass shootings
description: Mobile killing units and collaborating forces begin systematic mass murder behind the eastern front.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Jews in the Reich are forced to wear the yellow star
description: The yellow star becomes a compulsory marker of exclusion and control in Nazi territories.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Massacre at Babi Yar
description: German forces and auxiliaries murder tens of thousands of Jews near Kyiv in one of the largest mass shootings of the Holocaust.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Chełmno killing center begins operations
description: Chełmno becomes the first stationary Nazi killing center using gas vans for mass murder.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Wannsee Conference coordinates the Final Solution
description: Senior Nazi officials discuss administrative coordination of the policy to murder Europe's Jews.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Mass deportations from the Warsaw Ghetto begin
description: Nazi authorities begin sending Jews from Warsaw to Treblinka for murder.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Warsaw Ghetto Uprising erupts
description: Jewish resistance fighters mount a major armed revolt against German liquidation of the ghetto.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Mass deportation of Hungarian Jews begins
description: Hundreds of thousands of Hungarian Jews are deported, most to Auschwitz, in a matter of weeks.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Auschwitz is liberated
description: Soviet forces liberate Auschwitz, revealing the scale of Nazi mass murder to the world.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Germany surrenders in Europe
description: Nazi Germany's defeat ends the Holocaust, though survivors still face displacement and loss.

timeline_description: Major events in the persecution and murder of European Jews under Nazi rule from 1933 to the postwar reckoning.
title: Nuremberg Trials begin
description: International tribunals begin prosecuting major Nazi leaders for crimes including those tied to the Holocaust.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Cugnot builds a steam road vehicle
description: Nicolas-Joseph Cugnot constructs an early self-propelled road vehicle powered by steam.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Early internal-combustion experiments advance
description: Inventors such as de Rivaz explore combustion engines for self-propelled vehicles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Lenoir develops a practical gas engine
description: Étienne Lenoir's engine becomes an important step toward motor vehicles powered by internal combustion.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Otto perfects the four-stroke engine
description: Nikolaus Otto's four-stroke engine becomes a central foundation of later automobile design.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Karl Benz builds a practical gasoline automobile
description: Benz creates one of the first practical motorcars powered by a gasoline engine.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Benz receives patent for the Motorwagen
description: The Benz Patent-Motorwagen is widely recognized as a landmark in the history of the automobile.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Bertha Benz completes a pioneering long-distance drive
description: Bertha Benz's trip demonstrates the practicality and publicity value of the new automobile.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Paris-Rouen competition showcases automobiles
description: One of the first organized motor competitions helps prove the promise of automotive transport.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Duryea Motor Wagon Company begins U.S. production
description: The Duryea brothers help launch the American automobile industry.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Ford introduces the Model T
description: The Model T makes the automobile far more accessible to ordinary consumers.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Moving assembly line transforms production
description: Ford's use of the moving assembly line dramatically reduces manufacturing time and cost.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Model T production ends
description: The end of Model T production closes the most famous early chapter of mass automobility.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Citroën launches the Traction Avant
description: The Traction Avant popularizes advanced features such as front-wheel drive and unitary construction.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Volkswagen Beetle project takes shape
description: The Beetle becomes one of the defining global car designs of the twentieth century.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Automobile ownership surges in the postwar era
description: Rising incomes, suburbanization, and highway building make the car central to daily life in many countries.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Oil crisis reshapes car markets
description: The oil shock increases demand for smaller and more fuel-efficient vehicles worldwide.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Japanese automakers gain global leadership
description: Japanese firms expand worldwide through efficient manufacturing and strong quality control.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Toyota launches the Prius
description: The Prius becomes the first mass-produced hybrid car and a symbol of automotive efficiency.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Tesla Roadster boosts modern electric-car interest
description: The Roadster helps renew worldwide interest in long-range battery-electric vehicles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Automobile
timeline_description: Major technological and industrial milestones in the development of the automobile from steam vehicles to the electric era.
title: Electric vehicles become a central industry transition
description: Major automakers and governments commit to expanded electric-vehicle production and infrastructure.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Aristotle writes On the Soul
description: Aristotle's work offers one of the earliest systematic explorations of mind, perception, and behavior.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Descartes influences mind-body debate
description: René Descartes's dualist philosophy shapes later inquiry into consciousness, sensation, and the relation of mind to body.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Locke publishes An Essay Concerning Human Understanding
description: John Locke's theory of experience and association influences later psychological thought.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Galvani's work stimulates research on nerves
description: Studies of bioelectricity help connect the nervous system to experimental investigation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species
description: Evolutionary theory profoundly influences later thinking about behavior, adaptation, and emotion.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Fechner founds psychophysics
description: Gustav Fechner's work links measurable stimuli to sensation and helps establish experimental psychology.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Wundt opens the first psychology laboratory
description: Wilhelm Wundt establishes a laboratory at Leipzig that is conventionally marked as the beginning of psychology as a formal discipline.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Hall opens the first American psychology laboratory
description: G. Stanley Hall helps institutionalize psychology in the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: William James publishes The Principles of Psychology
description: James's major text helps define the scope and questions of modern psychology.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Lightner Witmer opens the first psychological clinic
description: Witmer's clinic in Pennsylvania helps launch clinical psychology as a profession.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Freud publishes The Interpretation of Dreams
description: Sigmund Freud's work popularizes psychoanalysis and influences twentieth-century views of the unconscious.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Binet and Simon develop an intelligence scale
description: The Binet-Simon test becomes a landmark in psychological measurement and educational assessment.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Watson launches behaviorism
description: John B. Watson argues that psychology should focus on observable behavior rather than introspection.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Piaget outlines stages of cognitive development
description: Jean Piaget's developmental theory becomes highly influential in psychology and education.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Rogers advances client-centered therapy
description: Carl Rogers's approach helps define humanistic and non-directive psychotherapy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Skinner publishes Science and Human Behavior
description: B. F. Skinner's work consolidates behaviorist theory and operant conditioning.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Cognitive revolution gathers force
description: Research in language, memory, and information processing shifts psychology toward cognitive models of mind.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Neisser's Cognitive Psychology helps define the field
description: Ulric Neisser's book gives coherence and visibility to the emerging cognitive approach.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: DSM-III reshapes psychiatric classification
description: The third edition of the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual standardizes diagnostic categories and influences clinical psychology.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Psychology
timeline_description: Major intellectual, experimental, and clinical milestones in the history of psychology from classical thought to modern cognitive science.
title: Neuroscience and brain imaging transform psychology
description: New imaging methods and cognitive neuroscience deepen the integration of psychology with biology.
